content:
# Revelation Chapter 12 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Book:** Revelation | **Chapter:** 12

**Verse 1 — "was seen" vs. "appeared"; "spectacular" vs. "great"; "under" vs. "beneath"; "having been clothed" vs. "clothed":**
- EMET: "a great sign was seen in the heaven: a woman having been clothed with the sun, and the moon under her feet"
- LOGOS: "a great sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, and the moon beneath her feet"
- PNEUMA: "a spectacular sign appeared in heaven: a woman clothed with the sun, with the moon beneath her feet"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Appeared" for ōphthē, "great" for mega (not "spectacular"), "clothed with" over the participial "having been clothed with," and "beneath" (LOGOS/PNEUMA majority) over "under."

**Verse 2 — "having [a child] in womb" vs. "with child" vs. "pregnant":**
- EMET: "having [a child] in womb, and she cries out, being in labor-pain and being tormented to give birth"
- LOGOS: "she was with child, and she cries out, being in labor and in pain to give birth"
- PNEUMA: "She was pregnant and cried out in the pain and agony of giving birth"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "With child" is the traditional rendering. PNEUMA's "pregnant" lacks formal register; "agony" interprets basanizomenē freely.

**Verse 3 — "fiery-red" vs. "red"; "diadems" vs. "royal crowns":**
- EMET: "a great fiery-red dragon...seven diadems"
- LOGOS: "a great red dragon...seven diadems"
- PNEUMA: "an enormous fiery-red dragon...seven royal crowns"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA adopts "fiery-red" (EMET/PNEUMA) — pyrros derives from pyr (fire). "Diadems" (EMET/LOGOS) preserves the distinction from stephanoi.

**Verse 4 — "drags" vs. "sweeps away" vs. "swept":**
- EMET: "his tail drags the third of the stars"
- LOGOS: "his tail sweeps away a third of the stars"
- PNEUMA: "His tail swept a third of the stars from the sky and hurled them to the earth"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA adopts "sweeps away" (LOGOS). PNEUMA shifts tense and adds "hurled."

**Verse 5 — "a male" vs. "a male child"; "shepherd" vs. "rule"; "rod" vs. "scepter"; "snatched up" vs. "caught up":**
- EMET: "a son, a male...shepherd...rod of iron...snatched up"
- LOGOS: "a son, a male child...shepherd...rod of iron...caught up"
- PNEUMA: "a son — a male child...rule...iron scepter...snatched away"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Shepherd" for poimainein (not "rule"). "Rod" for rhabdō (not "scepter"). "Caught up" preserves the theological resonance of harpazō.

**Verse 6 — Sentence structure; "nourish" vs. "take care of"; number format:**
- EMET: "she has there a place having been prepared...nourish her a thousand two hundred sixty days"
- LOGOS: "she has a place prepared...nourish her for one thousand two hundred and sixty days"
- PNEUMA: "God had prepared a place to take care of her for 1,260 days"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Nourish" for trephōsin. Written-out numbers for formal register.

**Verse 7 — "to make war" vs. "waging war" vs. "fought against"/"fought back":**
- EMET: "Michael and his angels [went] to make war...the dragon made war, and his angels"
- LOGOS: "Michael and his angels waging war...the dragon and his angels waged war"
- PNEUMA: "Michael and his angels fought against the dragon...the dragon and his angels fought back"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. PNEUMA's "fought back" adds narrative nuance not in the Greek.

**Verse 9 — "cast [out]" vs. "cast out" vs. "thrown down"; "inhabited world" vs. "world":**
- EMET: "was cast [out]...the one deceiving the whole inhabited world...cast to the earth"
- LOGOS: "was cast out...who deceives the whole inhabited world...cast out to the earth"
- PNEUMA: "was thrown down...the deceiver of the whole world...hurled to the earth"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Cast out" for eblēthē. "Inhabited world" for oikoumenē (more precise than "world").

**Verse 10 — "great voice" vs. "loud voice"; "Christos" vs. "Christ"; "brothers" vs. "brothers and sisters":**
- EMET: "a great voice...his Christos...our brothers was cast [down]"
- LOGOS: "a loud voice...his Christ...our brothers has been cast out"
- PNEUMA: "a loud voice...his Christ...our brothers and sisters has been thrown down"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Christ" for Christos. "Brothers" retained — adelphōn can encompass both genders but SOPHIA preserves the Greek form.

**Verse 11 — "overcame" vs. "conquered"; "did not love their life unto death" vs. "did not cling to their own lives":**
- EMET: "they overcame him...did not love their life unto death"
- LOGOS: "they overcame him...did not love their life even unto death"
- PNEUMA: "They conquered him...did not cling to their own lives, even in the face of death"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Overcame" for enikēsan. "Even unto death" faithfully renders achri thanatou. PNEUMA's "cling to" freely interprets ēgapēsan (loved).

**Verse 12 — "tabernacling" vs. "dwell" vs. "dwell"; "having great wrath" vs. "with great wrath" vs. "burning with fury":**
- EMET: "those tabernacling in them...having great wrath...he has [only] a short time"
- LOGOS: "those who dwell in them...with great wrath...he has but a short time"
- PNEUMA: "all who dwell in them...burning with fury...his time is short"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Dwell" over "tabernacling" (hyper-literal) and "celebrate" over "rejoice." "With great wrath" is faithful; "burning with fury" (PNEUMA) is dynamic.

**Verse 13 — "was cast" vs. "had been cast" vs. "had been thrown down"; "saw" vs. "realized":**
- EMET: "he was cast to the earth"
- LOGOS: "he had been cast to the earth"
- PNEUMA: "he had been thrown down...realized"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Had been cast" for past-perfect context. "Saw" (EMET/LOGOS) for eiden, not "realized" (PNEUMA).

**Verse 14 — "the great eagle" vs. "a great eagle":**
- EMET: "the two wings of the great eagle"
- LOGOS: "the two wings of the great eagle"
- PNEUMA: "the two wings of a great eagle"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows EMET/LOGOS — the Greek has the definite article (tou aetou tou megalou).

**Verse 15 — "cast out" vs. "spewed"; "carried-away-by-the-river" vs. "carried away by the flood":**
- EMET: "cast out of his mouth...make her carried-away-by-the-river"
- LOGOS: "cast out of his mouth...cause her to be carried away by the flood"
- PNEUMA: "spewed water from his mouth...sweep the woman away in a flood"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Cast out" for ebalen. "Carried away by the flood" for potamophorēton.

**Verse 17 — "seed" vs. "offspring" vs. "children"; "Yeshua" vs. "Jesus"; "having" vs. "hold" vs. "hold to":**
- EMET: "the rest of her seed...the testimony of Yeshua"
- LOGOS: "the rest of her offspring...hold the testimony of Jesus"
- PNEUMA: "the rest of her children...hold to the testimony of Jesus"
- *Resolution:* SOPHIA follows LOGOS. "Offspring" for spermatos. "Jesus" for Iēsou (standard English form). "Hold the testimony" for echontōn tēn marturian.

---

*Signed, SOPHIA — in wisdom, seeking the faithful center where precision meets clarity*
